questions.q: The concept of 'One Nation, One Election' was first suggested by?
questions.explanation: N. Chandrababu Naidu suggested the concept of 'One Nation, One Election' to reduce the burden on political parties and the Election Commission during elections.

questions.q: Which of the following is a feature of the National Register of Citizens (NRC)?
opts: (A) It is a database of all Indian citizens
(B) It is a database of all foreigners residing in India
(C) It is a database of all citizens and foreigners residing in India
(D) It is a database of only citizens of Assam
questions.explanation: The National Register of Citizens (NRC) is a database of all citizens of Assam, created to identify and verify the citizenship status of individuals in the state.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the Goods and Services Tax (GST)?
opts: (A) To reduce the tax burden on consumers
(B) To simplify the tax structure and reduce tax evasion
(C) To increase the tax revenue for the government
(D) To eliminate the need for multiple tax returns
questions.explanation: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) aims to simplify the tax structure, reduce tax evasion, and make the tax system more efficient.

questions.q: The 'Right to Information Act' was passed in which year?
questions.explanation: The Right to Information Act was passed in 2005 to provide citizens with the right to access information from government institutions and public authorities.

questions.q: What is the main purpose of the 'National Human Rights Commission (NHRC)'?
opts: (A) To promote human rights and social justice
(C) To provide compensation to victims of human rights violations
(D) To educate people about human rights
questions.explanation: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) is established to promote human rights and social justice, and to protect the rights of vulnerable sections of society.

questions.q: The 'National Security Act' was passed in which year?
questions.explanation: The National Security Act was passed in 1985 to empower the government to detain individuals suspected of threats to national security.

questions.q: The 'Panchayat (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act' was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Panchayat (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act was enacted in 1996 to provide for the better administration of scheduled areas.

questions.q: What is the main feature of the '73rd Constitutional Amendment'?
opts: (A) It empowers the state governments to abolish the panchayats
(B) It provides for the reservation of seats for SCs and STs in panchayats
(C) It provides for the creation of a separate department for panchayati raj
(D) It provides for the direct election of sarpanches
questions.explanation: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment provides for the reservation of seats for SCs and STs in panchayats, and for the creation of a separate department for panchayati raj.

questions.q: The 'National Commission to Review the Working of the Constitution' was set up in which year?
questions.explanation: The National Commission to Review the Working of the Constitution was set up in 2002 to review the working of the Constitution.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the 'National Judicial Commission'?
opts: (A) To promote judicial reforms and improve the efficiency of the judiciary
(B) To provide training to judges and judicial officers
(C) To increase the number of judges and judicial officers
(D) To reduce the workload of the judiciary
questions.explanation: The National Judicial Commission is established to promote judicial reforms and improve the efficiency of the judiciary.

questions.q: The 'Right to Education Act' was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Right to Education Act was enacted in 2009 to make education a fundamental right for all children.

questions.q: What is the main feature of the 'National Urban Employment Guarantee Act'?
opts: (A) It provides for a national employment guarantee for all urban workers
(B) It provides for a state-specific employment guarantee for all urban workers
(C) It provides for a national employment guarantee for all rural workers
(D) It provides for a state-specific employment guarantee for all rural workers
questions.explanation: The National Urban Employment Guarantee Act provides for a state-specific employment guarantee for all urban workers.

questions.q: The 'National Rural Employment Guarantee Act' was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The National Rural Employment Guarantee Act was enacted in 2006 to provide a guaranteed employment to all rural workers.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the 'National Food Security Act'?
opts: (A) To provide food security to all citizens
(B) To provide food security to only the poor and vulnerable sections of society
(C) To reduce the price of food grains in the market
(D) To increase the production of food grains in the country
questions.explanation: The National Food Security Act aims to provide food security to only the poor and vulnerable sections of society.

questions.q: The 'National Health Mission' was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: The National Health Mission was launched in 2006 to improve the health infrastructure and services in the country.

questions.q: What is the main feature of the 'National Environment Policy'?
opts: (A) It aims to protect the environment and conserve natural resources
(B) It aims to promote economic growth and development
(C) It aims to reduce the impact of climate change
(D) It aims to increase the use of non-renewable energy sources
questions.explanation: The National Environment Policy aims to protect the environment and conserve natural resources.

questions.q: The 'National Disaster Management Authority' was established in which year?
questions.explanation: The National Disaster Management Authority was established in 2005 to coordinate disaster management efforts in the country.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the 'National Commission for Scheduled Castes'?
opts: (A) To promote social justice and equality for SCs
(B) To provide education and employment opportunities to SCs
(C) To protect the rights of SCs and provide them with compensation
(D) To increase the representation of SCs in government jobs and politics
questions.explanation: The National Commission for Scheduled Castes is established to promote social justice and equality for SCs.

questions.q: The 'Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act' was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act was enacted in 2006 to protect women from domestic violence.
questions.topic: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act

questions.q: What is the main feature of the 'National Policy on Education'?
opts: (A) It aims to universalize elementary education and improve the quality of education
(B) It aims to increase the access to higher education and promote research
(C) It aims to reduce the burden of education on the government
(D) It aims to increase the use of technology in education
questions.explanation: The National Policy on Education aims to universalize elementary education and improve the quality of education.

questions.q: The 'National Commission for Scheduled Tribes' was established in which year?
questions.explanation: The National Commission for Scheduled Tribes was established in 1990 to protect the rights of STs.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the 'National Commission for Women'?
opts: (A) To promote women's empowerment and equality
(B) To provide education and employment opportunities to women
(C) To protect the rights of women and provide them with compensation
(D) To increase the representation of women in government jobs and politics
questions.explanation: The National Commission for Women is established to promote women's empowerment and equality.

questions.q: The 'National Human Rights Commission Act' was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The National Human Rights Commission Act was enacted in 1993 to establish the National Human Rights Commission.

questions.q: What is the main feature of the 'National Disaster Management Act'?
opts: (A) It provides for a national disaster management framework
(B) It provides for a state-specific disaster management framework
(C) It provides for a national emergency response system
(D) It provides for a state-specific emergency response system
questions.explanation: The National Disaster Management Act provides for a national disaster management framework.
